evidence: Relationship Hero, online dating/relationship coaching, was founded in 2017 (YC S17) by Liron Shapira and Lior Gotesman.
Opendoor, the data-driven iBuyer/house-flipping platform, was founded in 2014.
Made Renovation, turnkey bathroom renovation packages, launched in July 2019, before the March 2020 pitch.
Lucy, the tobacco-free nicotine gum subscription brand, was founded in 2016 and launched its gum product in 2019.
Thrive Market, an organic-products membership with wholesale-style discounts, was founded in 2014.
Thrive Market's 'Thrive Gives' program sponsors a free membership for a low-income family for every paid membership sold, a program running since the company's 2014 founding.

Sam Parr (via The Hustle) launched Trends.co in 2020, a paid newsletter/subscription of curated business ideas and founder insights, matching the pitched 'Trends book' concept; later sold to HubSpot in 2021 and sunset in 2024.
Large video-based test-prep ed-tech platforms targeting Indian students (Byju's founded 2011, Unacademy 2015) already existed well before the April 2020 pitch.

AI-powered accounts-receivable collection tools integrating with QuickBooks and automating email dunning have since been built by multiple companies, though not directly traceable to this specific pitch.
Generic idea about fixing retention in online test-prep education; no specific company traceable to this pitch.
